1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

36	## Current Objectives
37	
38	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.

40	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — complete Phase B via path (a) (`let M` named-comp abbreviation) to close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
41	
42	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L502, L826, L854 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1240 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1285 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1314 `h_loc_exact`.
43	
44	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1240) by completing Phase B (per-summand R-linearity) via **path (a)**: introduce a named `let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := ...` abbreviation **BEFORE** the iter-081 simp chain collapses the comp into a syntactic `∘ₗ`. After this, the goal becomes `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y))) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))`, which reduces to proving `M`'s R-linearity at the j-component via per-summand reasoning through the named definition (no need to split the comp syntactically).
45	
46	**State to build on (DO NOT modify).** These commits are essential prerequisites:
47	- `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` at L418 (iter-078).
48	- `intro r y; funext j` at L1102–1103 (iter-078).
49	- The named-per-i `letI` block at L920–949 (iter-080): `letI perI_n : ∀ i, Module R (Z_n i) := …; letI h_mod_pi_n : Module R (∀ i, Z_n i) := Pi.module _ _ _` for n = 1, 2, 3.
50	- **The iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain at L1120–1153**: `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]`; `have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n`; 5-layer `dsimp only`; full `simp [..., dif_pos hRel]`.
51	- **The iter-082 S5 prelude at L1161–1170**: `rw [show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂ from rfl, show ... = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁ from rfl]` re-fold + `rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply, ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply]`. After this prelude, the `j`-projection is adjacent to the comp.
52	
53	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i`, the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences. The R-action on `∀ i, Z_n i` is defined componentwise via `Pi.module`, where each `Z_n i = Γ(C.left, V_i)` is an R-algebra via the restriction map. The per-summand R-linearity reduces to: `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op).hom` composed with `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r` equals `(presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r` (`← presheaf.map_comp`); and a ring-hom factoring of the product via `RingHom.map_mul`.
54	
55	**Recipe (path (a) — named-comp abbreviation).**
56	
57	- **R0 — Introduce the named `M` abbreviation.** Just BEFORE the iter-081 S2+S3+S4 chain (so it sits at ~L1101, right after `intro r y; funext j`), or AT the location where the simp would expose `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ Σ_hom`, insert:
58	  ```lean
59	  -- Name the differential as a single LinearMap so subsequent simp doesn't expose it as `∘ₗ`
60	  let M : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁) →ₗ[k] ↑(∏ᶜ Z₂) := (eqToHom _).hom ∘ₗ
61	    (∑ i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2), (-1)^(↑i : ℤ) •
62	       LinearMap.pi (fun j' => (Pi.π Z₁ (j' ∘ ?δ_i.toOrderHom)) ≫
63	                                  (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (?φ_i j').op))
64	  ```
65	  (The precise syntactic shape needs adjusting — `Pi.lift` vs `LinearMap.pi`, the `δ_i`/`φ_i` references to whatever the surrounding context names them, etc. Use `lean_goal` after the S2+S3+S4 chain to read the exact shape of the sum, then construct `M` to match.)
66	- **R1 — Modify the iter-081 simp set** to NOT unfold `M`. The full `simp` invocation already unfolds `CochainComplex.of`, `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor`, etc.; just don't include `M` in the simp set. The `dif_pos hRel` step then exposes the alternating sum AS the body of `M`, so both sides of the goal become:
67	  ```
68	  (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y))) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))
69	  ```
70	- **R2 — Prove `M.map_smul'` per-summand.** Show `∀ (r : R) (y : ↑(∏ᶜ Z₁)), (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M (perI₁.smul r y)) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (M y)` (the R-linearity statement at the j-component for this specific `M`). The proof unfolds `M`, applies `Pi.π Z₂ j` past the sum via `LinearMap.coe_sum` + `Finset.sum_apply`, then per-summand:
71	  - `Pi.π Z₂ j` past `LinearMap.pi`/`Pi.lift` and `(toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op`.
72	  - `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom) = (perI₁ (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)).smul r (y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom))` via `Pi.smul_apply` + the named-per-i `perI₁` from iter-080.
73	  - Unfold `perI₁`'s smul: `(presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op).hom r * y (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)`.
74	  - Apply `RingHom.map_mul` on `(toModuleKPresheaf C).map (φ_i j).op . hom` to split the product.
75	  - Apply `← C.left.presheaf.map_comp` to collapse `presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_i}).op ∘ presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_i} ≤ U).op = presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op`.
76	  - RHS: outer `r •` at j-component matches `(perI₂ j).smul r _ = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * _`.
77	  - Both sides reduce to the same product; close per-summand by `rfl`, then `Finset.sum_congr rfl` closes the outer sum.
78	- **R3 — Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** by combining the R2 lemma with the post-S5 goal shape. The `eqToHom_app` cast on `M` is itself an `AddEquiv.module`-transport that commutes with R-smul (both source and target carry the same R-action via the same restriction map); the cast collapses on the j-component.
79	
80	**Partial-credit fallback.** If R2 succeeds but R3's `eqToHom`-cast collapse stalls, leave the cast-collapse as a `?_` placeholder and document the remaining goal. This does NOT reduce the file sorry count but gives iter-084 a clean handoff. Do NOT stop early without documenting precisely what failed.
81	
82	**Approaches confirmed dead-end iter-082** (do NOT re-attempt):
83	- `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` against homogeneous `∘ₗ` — fails (∘ₛₗ-vs-∘ₗ HOU mismatch).
84	- `simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]` — no progress (no explicit `⇑` to fire on).
85	- `change` to explicitly unfolded comp form — fails on the implicit `eqToHom ⋯` type proof.
86	- `dsimp only []` on the comp — no progress.
87	- `rfl` after the S5 prelude — sides not defeq (per-summand R-linearity is the construct, not a tautology).
88	- `{cone := …, isLimit := …}` brace syntax inside `show … from rfl` — parser fails (use `⟨_,_⟩` anonymous-constructor brackets).
89	- `exact map_smul (scK₀.f.hom) r _` — `scK₀.f` is k-linear, not R-linear.
90	- `module` tactic — not a pure linear-combination shape.
91	- `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` — does NOT exist (iter-081 verified; use `dif_pos hRel` in full simp instead).
92	
93	**Approaches ruled out earlier** (preserved compactly):
94	- `rw [Pi.smul_apply]` against anonymous-builder `h_mod_pi₂.toSMul` (pre-iter-080 refactor).
95	- `letI` parallel instance to bypass anonymity (iter-079).
96	- `simp only` (instead of full `simp`) for S2+S3+S4 (iter-081 confirmed fails).
97	- `rw [dif_pos hRel]` after a `simp only` chain (iter-081 confirmed).
98	
99	**Hard constraints**:

817	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
818	
819	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
820	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
821	```
822	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
823	```

825	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
826	compatibility above.

828	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
829	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
830	itself is closed by assembling them.

832	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
833	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
834	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
835	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
836	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
837	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
838	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
839	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
840	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
841	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
842	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
843	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
844	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩

848	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
849	being locally free of the given rank.

851	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
852	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
853	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
854	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
855	    Smooth f ↔
856	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
857	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
858	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
859	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
860	  sorry

868	`k`-vector space. -/

870	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
871	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
872	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :

874	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
875	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)

890	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
891	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
892	restriction of scalars. -/
893	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
894	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
895	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
896	    (M : C.left.Modules)
897	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
898	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
899	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
900	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
901	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
902	  -- naturality.
903	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
904	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
905	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
906	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
907	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
908	  -- Now goal:
909	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
910	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
911	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
912	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
913	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
914	    ext x
915	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
916	  rw [hpres]
917	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
918	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)

920	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
921	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
922	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
923	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
924	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
925	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
926	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]

928	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg

930	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
931	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
932	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
933	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
934	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
935	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
936	    rw [halg]
937	  rw [htarget] at hnat
938	  exact hnat

998	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
999	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
1000	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
1001	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
1002	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
1003	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
1004	
1005	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
1006	
1007	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
1008	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
1009	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
1010	
1011	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
1012	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
1013	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
1014	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
1015	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
1016	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
1017	      Module.rank k
1018	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
1019	  sorry
1020	
1021	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

13	
14	In this file, it is shown that the category of sheaves of modules over
15	a sheaf of rings `R` is an abelian category. More precisely,
16	if `J` is a Grothendieck topology on a category `C` and `R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}`,
17	then `SheafOfModules.{v} R` is abelian if the conditions `HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}`
18	and `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}` are satisfied.
19	
20	In particular, if `u = v` and `C : Type u` is a small category,
21	then `SheafOfModules.{u} R` is abelian: this instance shall be
22	found automatically if this file and `Algebra.Category.Grp.FilteredColimits`
23	are imported.

15	in the category of presheaves of modules.
16

28	  {M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} {f : M₁ ⟶ M₂}
29	
30	lemma epi_of_surjective (hf : ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Surjective (f.app X)) : Epi f where
31	  left_cancellation g₁ g₂ hg := by
32	    ext X m₂
33	    obtain ⟨m₁, rfl⟩ := hf m₂
34	    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom ((evaluation R X ⋙ forget _).congr_map hg) m₁
35	
36	lemma mono_of_injective (hf : ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Injective (f.app X)) : Mono f where
37	  right_cancellation {M} g₁ g₂ hg := by
38	    ext X m
39	    exact hf (ConcreteCategory.congr_hom ((evaluation R X ⋙ forget _).congr_map hg) m)
40	
41	variable (f)
42	
43	instance [Epi f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) : Epi (f.app X) :=
44	  inferInstanceAs (Epi ((evaluation R X).map f))
45	
46	instance [Mono f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) : Mono (f.app X) :=
47	  inferInstanceAs (Mono ((evaluation R X).map f))
48	
49	lemma surjective_of_epi [Epi f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
50	    Function.Surjective (f.app X) := by
51	  rw [← ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective]
52	  infer_instance
53	
54	lemma injective_of_mono [Mono f] (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
55	    Function.Injective (f.app X) := by
56	  rw [← ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
57	  infer_instance

59	lemma epi_iff_surjective :
60	    Epi f ↔ ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Surjective (f.app X) :=
61	  ⟨fun _ ↦ surjective_of_epi f, epi_of_surjective⟩

64	    Mono f ↔ ∀ ⦃X : Cᵒᵖ⦄, Function.Injective (f.app X) :=

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The presheaf of differentials of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, we define the type `M.Derivation φ` of derivations
15	with values in a presheaf of `R`-modules `M` relative to
16	a morphism of `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` of presheaves of commutative rings
17	over categories `C` and `D` that are related by a functor `F : C ⥤ D`.
18	We formalize the notion of universal derivation.
19	
20	Geometrically, if `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes (or more generally
21	a morphism of commutative ringed spaces), we would like to apply
22	these definitions in the case where `F` is the pullback functor from
23	open subsets of `S` to open subsets of `X` and `φ` is the
24	morphism $O_S ⟶ f_* O_X$.
25	
26	In order to prove that there exists a universal derivation, the target
27	of which shall be called the presheaf of relative differentials of `φ`,
28	we first study the case where `F` is the identity functor.
29	In this case where we have a morphism of presheaves of commutative
30	rings `φ' : S' ⟶ R`, we construct a derivation
31	`DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` which is universal.
32	Then, the general case (TODO) shall be obtained by observing that
33	derivations for `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` identify to derivations
34	for `S' ⟶ R` where `S'` is the pullback by `F` of the presheaf of
35	commutative rings `S` (the data is the same: it suffices
36	to show that the two vanishing conditions `d_app` are equivalent).
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	universe v u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
43	
44	open CategoryTheory
45	
46	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
47	
48	namespace PresheafOfModules
49	
50	variable {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {F : C ⥤ D} {S' R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
51	  (M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
52	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) (φ' : S' ⟶ R)
53	
54	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`,
55	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
56	@[ext]
57	structure Derivation where
58	  /-- the underlying additive map `R.obj X →+ M.obj X` of a derivation -/
59	  d {X : Dᵒᵖ} : R.obj X →+ M.obj X
60	  d_mul {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a b : R.obj X) : d (a * b) = a • d b + b • d a := by cat_disch
61	  d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
62	    d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x) := by cat_disch
63	  d_app {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj X) : d (φ.app X a) = 0 := by cat_disch
64	
65	namespace Derivation
66	
67	-- Note: `d_app` cannot be a simp lemma because `dsimp` would
68	-- simplify the composition of functors `R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`
69	attribute [simp] d_mul d_map
70	
71	variable {M N φ}
72	
73	lemma congr_d {d d' : M.Derivation φ} (h : d = d') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
74	    d.d b = d'.d b := by rw [h]
75	
76	variable (d : M.Derivation φ)
77	
78	@[simp] lemma d_one (X : Dᵒᵖ) : d.d (X := X) 1 = 0 := by
79	  simpa using d.d_mul (X := X) 1 1
80	
81	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
82	/-- The postcomposition of a derivation by a morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
83	@[simps! d_apply]
84	def postcomp (f : M ⟶ N) : N.Derivation φ where
85	  d := (f.app _).hom.toAddMonoidHom.comp d.d
86	  d_map {X Y} g x := by simpa using naturality_apply f g (d.d x)
87	  d_app {X} a := by
88	    dsimp
89	    erw [d_app]
90	    rw [map_zero]
91	
92	/-- The universal property that a derivation `d : M.Derivation φ` must
93	satisfy so that the presheaf of modules `M` can be considered as the presheaf of
94	(relative) differentials of a presheaf of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
95	structure Universal where
96	  /-- An absolute derivation of `M'` descends as a morphism `M ⟶ M'`. -/
97	  desc {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
98	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : M ⟶ M'
99	  fac {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
100	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : d.postcomp (desc d') = d' := by cat_disch
101	  postcomp_injective {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
102	    (φ φ' : M ⟶ M') (h : d.postcomp φ = d.postcomp φ') : φ = φ' := by cat_disch
103	
104	attribute [simp] Universal.fac
105	
106	instance : Subsingleton d.Universal where
107	  allEq h₁ h₂ := by
108	    suffices ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
109	      (d' : M'.Derivation φ), h₁.desc d' = h₂.desc d' by
110	        cases h₁
111	        cases h₂
112	        simp only [Universal.mk.injEq]
113	        ext : 2
114	        apply this
115	    intro M' d'
116	    apply h₁.postcomp_injective
117	    simp
118	
119	end Derivation
120	
121	/-- The property that there exists a universal derivation for
122	a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
123	class HasDifferentials : Prop where
124	  exists_universal_derivation : ∃ (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
125	      (d : M.Derivation φ), Nonempty d.Universal
126	
127	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ R`,
128	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
129	abbrev Derivation' : Type _ := M.Derivation (F := 𝟭 D) φ'
130	
131	namespace Derivation'
132	
133	variable {M φ'}
134	
135	@[simp]
136	lemma d_app (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a : S'.obj X) :
137	    d.d (φ'.app X a) = 0 :=
138	  Derivation.d_app d _
139	
140	/-- The derivation relative to the morphism of commutative rings `φ'.app X` induced by
141	a derivation relative to a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings. -/
142	noncomputable def app (d : M.Derivation' φ') (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X) :=
143	  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk (fun b ↦ d.d b)
144	
145	@[simp]
146	lemma app_apply (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
147	    (d.app X).d b = d.d b := rfl
148	
149	section
150	
151	variable (d : ∀ (X : Dᵒᵖ), (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X))
152	
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ'`, this is the
155	in derivation `M.Derivation' φ'` that is given by a compatible family of derivations
156	with values in the modules `M.obj X` for all `X`. -/
157	def mk (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
158	    (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x)) : M.Derivation' φ' where
159	  d {X} := AddMonoidHom.mk' (d X).d (by simp)
160	
161	variable (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
162	      (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x))
163	
164	@[simp]
165	lemma mk_app (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (mk d d_map).app X = d X := rfl
166	
167	/-- Constructor for `Derivation.Universal` in the case `F` is the identity functor. -/
168	def Universal.mk {d : M.Derivation' φ'}
169	    (desc : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
170	      (_ : M'.Derivation' φ'), M ⟶ M')
171	    (fac : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
172	      (d' : M'.Derivation' φ'), d.postcomp (desc d') = d')
173	    (postcomp_injective : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
174	      (α β : M ⟶ M'), d.postcomp α = d.postcomp β → α = β) : d.Universal where
175	  desc := desc
176	  fac := fac
177	  postcomp_injective := postcomp_injective
178	
179	end
180	
181	end Derivation'
182	
183	namespace DifferentialsConstruction
184	
185	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
186	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials of a morphism of presheaves of
187	commutative rings. -/
188	@[simps -isSimp]
189	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials' :
190	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
191	  obj X := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X)
192	  -- Have to hint `g' := R.map f` below, or it gets unfolded weirdly.
193	  map f := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map (g' := R.map f) (φ'.naturality f)
194	  -- Without `dsimp`, `ext` doesn't pick up the right lemmas.
195	  map_id _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
196	  map_comp _ _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
197	
198	attribute [simp] relativeDifferentials'_obj
199	
200	@[simp]
201	lemma relativeDifferentials'_map_d {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
202	    DFunLike.coe (α := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X))
203	      (β := fun _ ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app Y))
204	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((relativeDifferentials' φ').map f))
205	        (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d x) =
206	        CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d (R.map f x) :=
207	  CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_d (φ'.naturality f) _
208	
209	/-- The universal derivation. -/
210	noncomputable def derivation' : (relativeDifferentials' φ').Derivation' φ' :=
211	  Derivation'.mk (fun X ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D (φ'.app X))
212	    (fun _ _ f x ↦ (relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f x).symm)
213	
214	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
215	/-- The derivation `Derivation' φ'` is universal. -/
216	noncomputable def isUniversal' : (derivation' φ').Universal :=
217	  Derivation'.Universal.mk
218	    (fun {M'} d' ↦
219	      { app := fun X ↦ (d'.app X).desc
220	        naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (fun b ↦ by
221	          dsimp
222	          rw [ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d, Derivation'.app_apply]
223	          erw [relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f]
224	          simp) })
225	    (fun {M'} d' ↦ by
226	      ext X b
227	      apply ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d)
228	    (fun {M} α β h ↦ by
229	      ext1 X
230	      exact CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (Derivation.congr_d h))
231	
232	instance : HasDifferentials (F := 𝟭 D) φ' := ⟨_, _, ⟨isUniversal' φ'⟩⟩
233	
234	end DifferentialsConstruction
235	
236	end PresheafOfModules

### `cotangentExactSeqBeta` refactor

Modified to use the extracted helper:

```lean
noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqBeta (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f := by
  let φ1' := ...
  let φ2' := ...
  let η := (cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη f g).choose
  have hη : η ≫ φ2' = φ1' := (cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη f g).choose_spec
  ...
```

**Important nuance**: cannot use `obtain ⟨η, hη⟩ := cotangentExactSeqBeta_hη f g` inside
a `noncomputable def` body — `obtain` (which uses `cases` under the hood) fails with
"recursor `Exists.casesOn` can only eliminate into `Prop`" because the surrounding
type is `Type`. The `.choose`/`.choose_spec` pattern is the correct workaround.

## What did NOT land this iter

### `h_exact ∧ h_epi` of `cotangentExactSeq_structure`

Single absorbed `sorry` preserved at the new L517 (formerly L576). Per the iter-083 plan's
conditional clause, the alternative (Option C: stalkwise-only via `exact_iff_stalkwise`)
was *not* pursued either, because adding `exact_iff_stalkwise` as a free-floating sorry
without closing the `_structure` body would be a regression (5 → 6).

### Concrete attack record (iter-083)

Three attempts on `h_epi` via Route 2:

1. `convert _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`:
   produces an iff residual whose RHS is the FULL statement of `map_surjective`, i.e.
   ```
   Function.Surjective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((cotangentExactSeqBeta f g).val.app U)) ↔
     ∀ (R S B : Type _) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S] [CommRing B] [Algebra S B]
       [Algebra R B] [IsScalarTower R S B], Function.Surjective ⇑(KaehlerDifferential.map R S B B)
   ```
   The iff residual cannot be closed without identifying the descent with
   `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.map` for specific `R,S,B` choices — exactly the
   bundled-vs-unbundled bridge documented as a blocker by iter-081/iter-082.

2. `convert _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective using 1`:
   produces the same iff residual but with `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` exposed on LHS via
   `show ... .hom`. Identical blocker.

3. `exact _root_.KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`:
   type mismatch — `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` is a `∀ R S B ...` Π-type
   needing concrete instances which don't unify with the bundled `ConcreteCategory.hom`
   form.

### Underlying structural blocker

The descent `(isUniversal' φ1').desc d1 .app U` and `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map fac`
agree on the generators `d b` (both send `d b ↦ d b` by `desc_d` + `map_d` + the universal
property), so they should be equal by `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext`. However, the
identification requires:

- A `fac : η.app U ≫ (φ2'.app U) = (φ1'.app U) ≫ 𝟙 _` coherence, obtainable from
  `congr_arg (NatTrans.app · U) hη` + `Category.comp_id`.
- The bundled `Module ((relativeDifferentials f).val.obj U)` instance is over the
  `forget₂`-image ring `(X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj U`, NOT directly
  over `X.presheaf.obj U`. Submodule.span_induction over this bundled module fails.
- `algebraize [(φ1'.app U).hom, (φ2'.app U).hom, (η.app U).hom]` sets up
  Algebra/IsScalarTower instances for the unbundled types but they do not unify with
  the bundled instance — `algebraize` introduces fresh instances, the bundled
  `ModuleCat.of` carries instances from the `letI := f.hom.toAlgebra` block in
  `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential`'s body, and the two synthesise differently even
  when definitionally equal.

A future iter-084+ closure would need either:

- (a) A new Mathlib API `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` (bundled
  surjectivity), reducing this to its application.
- (b) A precise `KaehlerDifferential.ext`-based identification chain that survives
  the bundled-vs-unbundled instance gap. Requires careful sequencing of `letI := ...
  toAlgebra` *outside* the goal context.
- (c) A direct `Submodule.range = ⊤` argument via `KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation`,
  showing the range contains all `d b` and is therefore the whole module. The descent's
  action on `d b` is computable via `desc_d`. Same instance gap surfaces, but with a
  smaller surface.

## h_exact concrete obstacle

Beyond the bundled-vs-unbundled bridge, `h_exact` additionally requires:

- A `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` criterion: not in Mathlib. Defining it requires
  the `SheafOfModules.stalkFunctor`, which is also not in Mathlib (only `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor`
  exists at the abelian-group level — bridging to `SheafOfModules` requires the
  `ModuleCat`-stalk infrastructure which is absent).
- A stalk identification: for each `x : X.toTopCat`, the stalk
  `(relativeDifferentials f).stalk x ≅ _root_.KaehlerDifferential (𝒪_{S,(g∘f)(x)}) (𝒪_{X,x})`.
  Not in Mathlib.
- Ring-level exactness from `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`.

Per the iter-083 plan: **do NOT introduce `SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` as a
free-floating sorry** — that would be a regression. The iter-083 prover therefore did
not introduce it.

## Recommendation for iter-084+

**Phase B forward priority** (in order of tractability):

1. **`h_epi` closure via `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.span_range_derivation`** (Option c above).
   Skip the bundled-vs-unbundled identification entirely; show range contains all `d b`
   directly. Estimated 1-2 iters with careful `letI` sequencing.

2. **`h_exact` upstream**: requires `SheafOfModules.stalkFunctor` to be defined as a
   project-local helper (multi-iter task, likely 3-5 iters of Mathlib gap-fill). Alternative
   route via `_root_.KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` applied chart-by-chart
   on affine covers is also viable but requires substantial Phase A infrastructure.

3. **Downstream of `_structure`**: `cotangent_exact_sequence` already assembles to a
   sorry-free statement (iter-073), so no follow-on work needed there once `_structure`
   closes.
